items.title: Man kills seven of his children, and an eighth child, in Louisiana mass shooting
items.summary: The children, aged from one to 14 years old, were killed in a shooting that police are describing as a "domestic disturbance".

items.title: Oil prices rise after Trump says Iranian ship seized
items.summary: Energy markets have seen wild swings since the US and Israel attacked Iran on 28 February.

items.title: Tehran will never cede control of Strait of Hormuz, senior Iranian politician tells BBC
items.summary: Lyse Doucet speaks to Ebrahim Azizi, who says Iran "will decide the right of passage" through the crucial shipping route.

items.title: Ukraine police chief resigns after officers allegedly fled deadly shooting
items.summary: Authorities say the officers have been suspended and an investigation into their actions is under way.

items.title: Rat poison found in HiPP baby food jar in Austria, police say
items.summary: The brand warned on Saturday that consuming the purées may be potentially "life-threatening".

items.title: Rumen Radev's party wins Bulgarian election
items.summary: With 87% of the vote now counted, Progressive Bulgaria has secured a majority in parliament.

items.title: Thousands of Parisians evacuated as WW2 bomb detonated
items.summary: Residents within a 450m radius of the device were told to leave their homes while it was neutralised.

items.title: Ontario Premier Doug Ford agrees to sell $21m private jet following outcry
items.summary: After confirming the purchase on Friday, Ford drew critcism from political opponents who called it a "gravy plane" and "out of touch".

items.title: Zelensky condemns US extension of Russian sanctions waiver
items.summary: The US argues that the waiver is meant to ease the energy supply crunch sparked by the US-Israel war with Iran.

items.title: Pope says 'tyrants' speech was not aimed at Trump
items.summary: The pontiff says his remarks have been misinterpreted after a spat with the US president.

items.title: Alec Baldwin to face civil trial over Rust film set shooting
items.summary: The criminal case against the actor was dismissed in 2024.

items.title: Bodies of 50 infants dumped at Trinidad graveyard
items.summary: Police say a preliminary investigation shows it may be a case of an "unlawful disposal of unclaimed corpses".

items.title: Australia's most-decorated soldier vows to fight war crime charges
items.summary: Ben Roberts-Smith has given his first statement since he was charged with five counts of the war crime of murder last week.

items.title: Mexico's Sheinbaum denies 'diplomatic crisis' with Spain after conquest row
items.summary: The Mexican president says there were never tensions over Spanish colonisation, which had become a thorny issue.

items.title: Building of Trump's White House ballroom can resume in full, appeals court says
items.summary: Construction at the White House is expected to continue until the next hearing, due in June.

items.title: At least six killed in Kyiv as gunman opens fire and takes hostages
items.summary: The attacker, who took hostages inside a supermarket, was killed after a shoot-out with police, officials say.

items.title: French peacekeeper killed in southern Lebanon
items.summary: President Macron blames the attack on Hezbollah. The Iran-backed armed group denies "any connection" to the incident.

items.title: Madonna joins Sabrina Carpenter to surprise Coachella
items.summary: The Queen of Pop makes a guest appearance during the Espresso singer's headline set.

items.title: The insider trading suspicions looming over Trump's presidency
items.summary: The BBC has found a pattern of spikes in trades ahead of public announcements by the US president.

items.title: 'They told me he was dead': Children born near army base learn truth about UK soldier dads
items.summary: A DNA and legal project has identified the fathers of 20 children born near a military base in Kenya.

items.title: India has splurged billions on metro trains. But where are the commuters?
items.summary: Without better last-mile connectivity and affordable fares, metro use is unlikely to improve quickly, say experts.

items.title: Fuel rations and free buses: How countries have responded to rising oil prices
items.summary: Governments around the world have introduced measures to limit the impact of price increases.

items.title: Art on trial - a sculptor's arrest highlights new extremes for censorship in China
items.summary: Gao Zhen is being retroactively punished for 15 year-old-works, in a case that has alarmed rights groups.

items.title: Why your recycled clothes could end up in this South American desert
items.summary: Old garments from around the world are being discarded in the South American country.

items.title: 'Croatia, but cheaper': The quirky holiday spots on trend for 2026
items.summary: Montenegro and Albania are among the places rising in popularity for Britons seeking a holiday away from tourist traps.

items.title: Watch: Trapped boy dangles from moving bus in Australia
items.summary: The boy, who was uninjured, was dragged for 350 metres after the back doors trapped his arm and school bag.

items.title: US releases video of forces seizing Iranian ship
items.summary: The US has intercepted an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Gulf as part of its naval blockade, Donald Trump has said.

items.title: Widespread damage as storm spreads through midwestern US
items.summary: A series of strong winds has torn through the midwestern US, ripping roofs off homes and leaving roads obstructed.

items.title: Drone footage shows huge Malaysian coastal village fire
items.summary: Thousands of people have been displaced after a fire destroyed around 1,000 homes in Malaysia's Sabah state.

items.title: Watch: Obama and Mamdani sing with children in New York
items.summary: The former US president and New York City mayor meet for the first time at a child care centre in the Bronx.

items.title: Watch: Runners v robots at China half marathon
items.summary: Robots competed in a half marathon race in Beijing on Sunday, with the winning machine leaving its human rivals for dust.
